prompt: hey can you turn this transcript into a readable article? it's from a youtube video about vector databases. the file is at ~/.claude/skills/yt-transcript-to-article-workspace/test-data/solo-tech-talk.txt

assertions.name: has_descriptive_title
assertions.description: Article has a descriptive, engaging title (not just 'Vector Databases')

assertions.name: has_tldr
assertions.description: Article has a TL;DR section near the top

assertions.name: has_thematic_sections
assertions.description: Article is organized by theme with section headings, not chronologically

assertions.name: has_key_takeaways
assertions.description: Article has a Key Takeaways section

assertions.name: no_timestamps
assertions.description: No timestamps (e.g. 0:00, 1:23) appear in the output

assertions.name: no_filler_words
assertions.description: No filler words like 'um', 'uh' remain in the article

assertions.name: mentions_all_databases
assertions.description: All 4 databases mentioned: Pinecone, Weaviate, ChromaDB/Chroma, pgvector

assertions.name: mentions_embedding_importance
assertions.description: Discusses that embedding model choice matters more than database choice

assertions.name: valid_markdown
assertions.description: Output is valid markdown with headers
